title: benthamia intermedia
intro: Benthamia intermedia, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The plant's roots and leaves are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, with the health benefits including pain relief and immune system support. Herbal preparations of Benthamia intermedia can be found in the form of tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
